commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ab3b684b2feaddaf9f2f6cec88a05c82b947d60e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a dedicated Active Memory recall lane, passes it to `runEmbeddedAgent`, and adds a regression assertion for that lane.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +14. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main calls Active Memory recall without a lane, and the embedded runner defaults missing lanes to `main`, which can self-block when called from a parent prompt-build hook already occupying that lane.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the intended Active Memory lane scope and require current-head real behavior proof; automation should not choose that scheduling policy.

target.title: fix(active-memory): isolate recall lane
target.body:
## Summary

Active Memory recall could block itself during prompt building.
The parent reply and the recall subagent could use the same queue lane, so recall waited behind the work that was waiting for recall.
This change puts recall on its own `active-memory` lane and adds a regression test for that call.

Fixes #79026.
Related: #72015.

## What Changed

The runtime change is intentionally small.
Recall still runs during `before_prompt_build`; it just no longer competes with the parent session lane.

- Added a named `active-memory` recall lane.
- Passed that lane to `runEmbeddedAgent` for Active Memory recall.
- Added a test that asserts the embedded recall call includes `lane: "active-memory"`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Active Memory recall no longer shares the parent main lane during prompt-build recall.

Real environment tested: Local macOS cleanup worktree for the focused unit regression. The existing PR proof also reported a local dev gateway run with `pnpm gateway:dev`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, Active Memory enabled, Memory Core enabled, and a local OpenAI-compatible loopback model endpoint.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts -t "runs recall on a dedicated active-memory lane"
```

Evidence after fix: The focused regression passed: 1 test passed, 126 skipped in `extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`. The existing gateway proof reported HTTP 200, Active Memory `start`, and Active Memory `done status=no_relevant_memory` in about 1.1s, with no prompt-build timeout.

Observed result after fix: The Active Memory recall call now passes `lane: "active-memory"`, so recall can run while the parent session is still building the prompt.

What was not tested: This cleanup did not rerun a hosted external model or real channel canary. An AWS Crabbox focused rerun was attempted, but the box lost network/DNS/SSH during the run (`run_f1a17d6fa03a`, lease `cbx_d5bb40661343`), so it was not counted as passing proof.

## Risks

Risk is low because this only changes the queue lane for Active Memory's embedded recall subagent.
It does not change memory search behavior, provider selection, channel delivery, or plugin defaults.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
